assessment_items.question_content:
**The dome is the crowning achievement of Hagia Sophia, and in its ambitious scale, it recalls the engineering brilliance of the . . .**


![Isidore of Miletus and Anthemius of Tralles (architects), Hagia Sophia, Istanbul, 532–37](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/fabe071607101b35b7a668ce3ca818bf.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**Justianian and other Byzantine emperors saw themselves as Romans and inheriting the Ancient Roman Empire.**

**[Read this essay to review](/humanities/art-history/art-history-400-1300-medieval---byzantine-eras/byzantine/a/hagia-sophia-istanbul)**

choices.content: Dome of the Rock in Jerusalem

assessment_items.question_content:
**Hagia Sophia became a symbol of the strength of Constantine the Great.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**Hagia Sophia became a symbol of the Byzantine Empire.**

**[Read this essay to review](/humanities/art-history/art-history-400-1300-medieval---byzantine-eras/byzantine/a/hagia-sophia-istanbul)**

assessment_items.question_content:
**Procopius, the biographer of the the Emperor Justinian, described the dome of Hagia Sophia as looking as though it was . . .**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**Procopius wrote that the dome of Hagia Sophia looked as though it was "suspended from heaven."**

**[Read this essay to review](/humanities/art-history/art-history-400-1300-medieval---byzantine-eras/byzantine/a/hagia-sophia-istanbul)**

assessment_items.question_content:
**What element of the capitals in Hagia Sophia reflect the general shift away from the solidity of Greek and Roman architecture?**

![Basket Capital, Hagia Sophia](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4a650773ae2c237e05ccc97f6262bbea.png)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**The capitals at Hagia Sophia are a hybrid of Roman composite capitals and Byzantine invention. They used drillwork to create a  thin and dematerialized effect contradicting their support function.**

**[Read this essay to review](/humanities/art-history/art-history-400-1300-medieval---byzantine-eras/byzantine/a/hagia-sophia-istanbul)**

choices.content: They introduced curved volutes as a decorative feature of the capitals.
They do not serve any structural function in the building.
They used drillwork to create a  thin and dematerialized effect contradicting their support function.
They added painted decoration to obscure the solid stone material.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following two architectural forms best describes the plan of Hagia Sophia?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Hagia Sophia combines elements of a basilica plan with a central plan on an vast scale.

**[Watch this video to review](/humanities/medieval-world/byzantine1/constantinople-east/v/hagia-sophia-istanbul)**

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which architectural element does NOT serve a structural function to support the dome of Hagia Sophia?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The triangular pedentives are necessary to support the round dome on the square structure beneath.

The windows beneath the dome do not support the dome.  They function symbolically to admit light and contribute to the floating effect of the structure.  

**[Watch this video to review](/humanities/medieval-world/byzantine1/constantinople-east/v/hagia-sophia-istanbul)**

choices.content:
![The windows at the base](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2f5c4e1dbecd23a008d8d5edfaa33499.jpg)
The windows at the base

![Vertical piers behind the pendentives](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ce8fdb401b5d1e943628d231839cad80.gif)
Vertical piers behind the pendentives
